name: Bohemia
startIntroPart1: George of Podebrad (also known as Jiří Podebrad) was the sixteenth King of Bohemia, who ruled in 1458–1471. He was a leader of the Hussites, but moderate and tolerant toward the Catholic faith. His rule was marked by great efforts to preserve peace and tolerance between the Hussites and Catholics in the religiously divided Crown of Bohemia.
startIntroPart2: Nowadays, in period of the so-called Czech National Revival, he began to be praised as the best Czech national monarch, a great diplomat and a courageous fighter against the domination of the Catholic Church and other civilizations that dream of getting Prague into their possession.
declaringWar: It's time to put an end to the blemish on this map. What blemish? Your failed state.
attacked: Your actions alone make you look like an arrogant fool.
defeated: Instead of trying the cake, you decided to snack on the whole city. Pity. The cake is delicious, by the way. If you know what I mean.
introduction: Greetings. I'm Jiří Podebrad. But my name is also Georg. I hope we will find a common language.
neutralHello: My people have spoken highly of you.
hateHello: I had a higher opinion of you.
hateNo: We are not agreeing to that!
afterPeace: Peace is a virtue of civilization, war is its crime. Finally, sanity has visited you!
tradeRequest: Don't worry, if you don't like this deal. We can soften the terms of the deal.
uniques: [+1 Science] from every specialist [in all cities]
[+1 Culture] from every specialist [in all cities]

name: America
startIntroPart1: Welcome President Washington! You lead the industrious American civilization! Formed in the conflagration of revolution in the 18th century, within a hundred years, the young nation became embroiled in a terrible civil war that nearly tore the country apart, but it was just a few short years later in the 20th century that the United States reached the height of its power, emerging triumphant and mighty from the two terrible wars that destroyed so many other great nations. The United States is a nation of immigrants, filled with optimism and determination. They lack only a leader to help them fulfill their promise.
startIntroPart2: President Washington, can you lead the American people to greatness? Can you build a civilization that will stand the test of time?
declaringWar: Your wanton aggression leaves us no choice. Prepare for war!
attacked: You have mistaken our love of peace for weakness. You shall regret this!
defeated: The day...is yours. I hope you will be merciful in your triumph.
introduction: The people of the United States of America welcome you.
hateNo: We are not agreeing to that!
afterPeace: Democracy will visit you again. But for now, you can be content with our mercy.
tradeRequest: Is the following trade of interest to you?
uniques: [-50]% Gold cost of acquiring tiles [in all cities]
Can transfer the Lend-Lease <after discovering the [Replaceable Parts]> to countries with which relations are [30]

name: Nitra
startIntroPart1: Pribina was the first known ruler of the Nitran Principality, a Slavic state in the territory of present-day Slovakia. He ruled from around 825 to 833, when he was expelled by Mojmír I of Moravia. Pribina later became a loyal vassal of the East Frankish Kingdom and founded the Balaton Principality.
startIntroPart2: Under Pribina's rule, Nitra was a center of early Slavic Christianity and trade. His legacy is one of resilience, diplomacy, and the struggle to maintain independence between the great powers of the Frankish Empire and the Bulgarian Khanate. Will you lead the Nitran people to glory, or will they be swallowed by larger neighbors once again?
declaringWar: By the time the sluggish heralds of your little blot on the map finally reach you, it will be far too late when you learn a few things. First, it seems my warriors have taken a pleasant stroll near your lands... You do not mind, do you? Ah, have I wounded your pride and breached your so-called sovereignty? Oh, who could possibly care, when, secondly, the entire world has now learned of your foul vices—not without my generous assistance. No soul loves you... Now, fall to your knees and kiss my feet.
attacked: Nothing but bravado... What total nonsense you’re spouting. You'd better look at yourself. Granted, I wasn't there to see it, but rumor has it the ladies are quite vexed with your... unconventional pastimes. In any case, it seems I must teach you a lesson.
defeated: A Pyrrhic victory, is it not? Look around you: you stand alone amidst ruins that no soul desires, and you yourself are wanted by none. A worthless wretch, who slaughtered hundreds... and for what? For sweet fantasies, to stroke your own fragile pride? Did it bring you some foul, solitary pleasure to watch the walls of my capital crumble? Hmph... I pray your wretched lineage ends with you. Though, judging by your miserable luck with women, that is already a certainty. The final word is mine, you pathetic prattler... you pygmy.
introduction: Another milksop on my hands... And we are to be acquainted, then? Plainly, today is not my day... I am Pribina. I have no fondness for polite chatter, yet I am never at a loss for words. So, if you are no mere prattler, do me a kindness and dispel my boredom with something of true worth.
neutralHello: Do not force me to waste my breath yet again. Unless, of course, you are a masochist who delights in being bathed in new, scathing titles. Or, perhaps, if your visit actually holds some shred of merit...
neutralNo: That is not in our interest.
hateHello: Red flags, you say?.. Those are my ducal banners. Now, what business have you here? What is that wretched squeaking I hear?.. Ah, it is you, the rat that scuttles beneath my feet... Shoo now, before I choke the life out of you.
hateLetsHearIt: I have better things to do.
hateYes: Fine. But this is the last time.
Very well, but I will remember this.
afterPeace: Peace is a fragile flower in these lands. Let us not trample it again.
tradeRequest: This is a fair offer, I assure you. Our highland goods are worth their weight in gold.
